topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

The crisp paper felt electric beneath my fingertips as I unfolded the document. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that seemed to fill the entire room. Every line, every carefully chosen anecdote, was undeniably *me*. I traced the bolded sections with a trembling finger, a wide, almost giddy smile spreading across my face. This wasn't just a history; it was a vibrant, meticulously crafted celebration of my life, a narrative I hadn't even known existed. "Oh my goodness," I whispered, the sound light and breathless. I bounced on the balls of my feet, a restless energy bubbling up inside me. I started laughing, a bright, unrestrained sound that echoed off the quiet walls. How could anyone have captured this kaleidoscope of experiences so perfectly?

[story 2]

I stared at the printed pages, my eyes scanning the text as if searching for a hidden secret, only to find an overwhelming sense of affirmation. A warmth flooded my chest, spreading outward like sunlight. I let out a short, sharp burst of laughter, shaking my head slightly as I reread a passage detailing a moment I’d only vaguely remembered. My hands moved quickly, turning pages with an almost frantic eagerness. A grin stretched across my face, wide and uncontainable. "This is incredible," I murmured, my voice pitched high with sheer delight. I sprang up from my chair, pacing the small space, my movements quick and light, as if ready to run toward the next discovery. The sheer scope of the narrative made my heart pound with joyful anticipation.

[story 3]

When the final paragraph came into view, a genuine, unrestrained cheer escaped my lips. I dropped the folder onto the desk with a soft thud, unable to keep still. My whole body seemed to vibrate with a sudden, buoyant energy. I looked up, my eyes shining, and a dazzling smile broke across my features. "Look at this!" I exclaimed, the tone of my voice ringing with pure, unadulterated joy. I clapped my hands together once, sharply, then twice, my energy manifesting in the way I stood—shoulders back, head tilted back slightly, completely buoyant. I began to pace back and forth, a quick, light skip in my steps, utterly thrilled by the sheer artistry of the story laid out before me.
